title: Topics of the land
preface: Topics are portable handles—places where story, civic work, and craft recognition meet. They resemble the stages of shaping a public identity without requiring that vocabulary at the surface.

axes.label: Public promise
axes.blurb: What the land claims out loud—and what breaks when the claim is clean but false.

axes.label: Voice & register
axes.blurb: How the tale is allowed to sound: grave, playful, administrative, interruptive.

axes.label: Who receives
axes.blurb: Who feels warmth when the promise lands—and who goes cold when it fails.

axes.label: Living memory
axes.blurb: What is filed as inevitable versus what returns as echo when the file is too clean.

axes.label: Held polarity
axes.blurb: Competing goods under one weather—generosity, constraint, and structure that must share a table.

axes.label: Motif system
axes.blurb: Recurring objects that travel across chambers the way a visual language travels across surfaces.

axes.label: Release rhythm
axes.blurb: Trust through return: a calendar the audience can count on.

axes.label: Marked funding
axes.blurb: How support appears in public without claiming ownership of the canon.

axes.label: Motivation & reward
axes.blurb: Where energy flows: immediate blueberries, delayed kitchen light, status treats, and pack warmth—especially across ages of the pack.

axes.label: Regenerative value
axes.blurb: How present need, productive reserves, shared resilience, and protected experiment remain visible on one living balance sheet.

axes.label: Experience path
axes.blurb: How claims, lived moments, evidence, memory, and continuation remain connected as a public identity develops.

axes.label: Guide figure
axes.blurb: A canine witness—Spwashi’s dog in fantasy dress—whose motives stay animal-clear: scent, fruit, pack, chase with consequence.
